items.title: Why Chinese exporters and shipping firms remain wary despite the US-Iran peace deal
items.summary:
Iran and the United States may have entered a temporary ceasefire, but uncertainty over the agreement continues to cast a shadow over the operations of Chinese businesses across the Gulf region.
While the deal has raised hopes that business activity could gradually resume, many traders and investors remain reluctant to make long-term commitments.

items.title: Malaysia demands US$257 million from Norwegian firm over axed missile deal
items.summary:
Malaysia is seeking compensation of 226 million euros (US$257 million) from a Norwegian defence firm after a deal to deliver a naval missile system was cancelled, despite the Southeast Asian country having paid about 95 per cent of the contract value.
The claim comprised 129.86 million euros in direct losses, including payments already made by the government, and 96.26 million euros for additional
